name: length_unchanged_after_remove
code:
proof fn phi_1_length_unchanged_after_remove<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(old_self: StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, post_self: StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, k: K, i: usize)
    requires
        old_self.valid(),
        i < old_self@.len(),
        post_self.valid(),
        k == old_self@.index(i as int),
        post_self@ == old_self@.remove(i as int),
        post_self@.to_set() == old_self@.to_set().remove(k),
    ensures
        post_self@.len() == old_self@.len(),
{
}
reason: Remove must decrease the length by exactly one; an unchanged length means the element was not actually removed.

name: singleton_remove_still_nonempty
code:
proof fn phi_2_singleton_remove_still_nonempty<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(old_self: StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, post_self: StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, k: K)
    requires
        old_self.valid(),
        old_self@.len() == 1,
        k == old_self@.index(0),
        post_self.valid(),
        post_self@ == old_self@.remove(0),
        post_self@.to_set() == old_self@.to_set().remove(k),
    ensures
        post_self@.len() > 0,
{
}
reason: Removing the only element from a singleton vec must yield an empty vec; a non-empty result would violate basic remove semantics.

name: removed_element_still_in_set
code:
proof fn phi_3_removed_element_still_in_set<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(old_self: StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, post_self: StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, k: K, i: usize)
    requires
        old_self.valid(),
        i < old_self@.len(),
        k == old_self@.index(i as int),
        post_self.valid(),
        post_self@ == old_self@.remove(i as int),
        post_self@.to_set() == old_self@.to_set().remove(k),
    ensures
        post_self@.to_set().contains(k),
{
}
reason: The returned element k was removed; if it still appears in the post-state set, the removal had no effect on the set representation.

name: set_unchanged_after_remove
code:
proof fn phi_4_set_unchanged_after_remove<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(old_self: StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, post_self: StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, k: K, i: usize)
    requires
        old_self.valid(),
        i < old_self@.len(),
        k == old_self@.index(i as int),
        post_self.valid(),
        post_self@ == old_self@.remove(i as int),
        post_self@.to_set() == old_self@.to_set().remove(k),
    ensures
        post_self@.to_set() =~= old_self@.to_set(),
{
}
reason: If the set view is identical before and after remove, the operation is a no-op at the set level, contradicting the purpose of remove.

name: old_set_subset_of_new_set
code:
proof fn phi_5_old_set_subset_of_new_set<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(old_self: StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, post_self: StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, k: K, i: usize)
    requires
        old_self.valid(),
        i < old_self@.len(),
        k == old_self@.index(i as int),
        post_self.valid(),
        post_self@ == old_self@.remove(i as int),
        post_self@.to_set() == old_self@.to_set().remove(k),
    ensures
        old_self@.to_set().subset_of(post_self@.to_set()),
{
}
reason: The old set is strictly larger (it contains k which was removed); if it were a subset of the new set, no element was actually lost.
